seoTitle: Myrtleville vs Myuna Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Myrtleville and Myuna Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Myrtleville vs Myuna Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Myrtleville and Myuna Bay.
heading: Myrtleville vs Myuna Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Myrtleville, New South Wales and Myuna Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Myrtleville has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Myuna Bay (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Myrtleville or Myuna Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Myrtleville has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Myuna Bay scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Myrtleville scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Myuna Bay scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Myrtleville or Myuna Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Myrtleville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
